titlestmt.titleproper: Robert S. and Eloise C. Wickham papers8408
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-01-25 18:26:33 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Robert S. and Eloise C. Wickham papers
did.physdesc: Bound manuscripts, correspondence, photographs, printed material, publications
did.abstract: Carpenter-Mayhew papers include correspondence, pamphlets, photographs, legal and estate papers, photographs, and clippings relating to Alvin R. Carpenter, Alice Carpenter, Charles Mayhew, Eloise Carpenter Wickham, and Robert Sloane Wickham. Charles Mayhew Carpenter papers include personal and business correspondence, 1910-1918, including letters from his estranged wife Mary.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Robert Sloane Wickham was born at Ballston Spa on March 14, 1877. He graduated from Saratoga Springs High School, studied law in the office of Babcock, sperry, and Van Cleve in Binghamton and attended Binghamton High School. In 1897, he won a state scholarship to Cornell and graduated in 1902 with a Bachelor of Law degree. In 1905 he married Eloise Cameron Carpenter. In 1908 they returned to Schuylerville, where he opened a law practice. In 1911, they went back to Binghamton when he was offered a junior partnership with the Chief Counsel of the Excise Department of New York State. He was an avid hiker and wrote two books, Friendly Adirondack Peaks and A Saratoga Boyhood (illustrated by Anne Allaben).

Eloise Cameron Carpenter was born in in Schuylerville, N.Y., the daughter of Alvin Rich Carpenter and Alida Catherine Mayhew. Alida Mayhew was the daughter of Charles Wadsworth Mahew and Maria DeRidder. Maria was the daughter of Walter DeRidder and Alida Vandenburgh. Walter DeRidder was the son of Simon DeRidder, a Brigadier General during the War of 1812 and Maria VAn Schaick. Eloise Carpenter Wickham graduated from Smith College. She had one brother, Charles Mayhew Carpenter who married Mary Alice Hendy of Cynthiana, Ky. They had two children Alvin Rich and Alice. When Charles and Mary Alice divorced Alice stayed with her mother and Charles returned to Binghamton to live with his aunt and uncle. Alvin graduated from Cornell in 1928..

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Robert S. and Eloise C. Wickham papers, #8408. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Carpenter-Mayhew papers include correspondence, pamphlets, photographs, legal and estate papers, photographs, and clippings relating to Alvin R. Carpenter, Alice Carpenter, Charles Mayhew, Eloise Carpenter Wickham, and Robert Sloane Wickham. Charles Mayhew Carpenter papers include personal and business correspondence, 1910-1918, including letters from his estranged wife Mary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Class of 1928 Reunion jacket and hats belonging to Alvin Rich Carpenter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Oversized items include DeRidder family slave indentures, commissions, officers' roll of Col. Walter DeRidder's Regiment, New York State Militia; Abiel W. Mayhew commission, Columbia Light Infantry, 1809; certificates and diplomas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Bound volumes include Catherine S. DeRidder travel diary, 1877; Eloise Wickham account books, 1932-1937; Robert S. Wickham physics notebook, undated; album of original drawings by Anne E. Allaben for Robert S. Wickham's book, A Saratoga Boyhood, with final proofs; and book of reviews of A Saratoga Boyhood. Robert Wickham's photograph albums depicting hiking, camping, hunting, and fishing in the Adirondacks, 4 volumes, 1905-1949, with negatives. Another Robert Wickham album depicting family photos, ca. 1860-1956. Also Charles Mayhew Carpenter photographs of family members, houses, and scenes, circa 1900-1914; a carte-de-visit album, probably Alvin and Alida Carpenter; Eloise Wickham school exercises, undated; Regimental record book with orders, muster rolls, and returns, Simon DeRidder, 1812; and various published books and pamphlets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlets, 1946-50 and n.d. Includes a history, 1896-1946, a map, 1950 greetings, 1950 general information and rules, and two copies of a much earlier pamphlet, The Back Log Idea.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, n.d., to "papa."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cabinet photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Estate papers, 1914

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter sent, 1914.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters received, 1914-16 and 1995. Most of the early letters were set by his mother, Mary (Hendy) Carpenter, or his sister, Alice Carpenter, while they were apart due to his parents' separation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters sent, 1917 and n.d. A letter to "Mrs. Landon" written at Oak Street School, 1917, apparently a class exercise; and an undated note to "papa" while he was still with his mother, opening with "I am verry lonesome with out you."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter sent, 1964, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham; family news.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph, unmounted, of Alice and Alvin Carpenter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters received, 1914, on insurance matters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Genealogical notes; a memo, "Who is Who," by C[atherine] S. DeRidder, Memorial Day, 1905; and a letter, George W. DeRidder, Ballston Spa, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, Binghamton, 15 November 1905.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, N.F. McClure, Washington, D.C., to Alvin R. Carpenter, Binghamton, 26 May 1941, enclosing a typescript of a genealogical supplement of the Hendy family of Kentucky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Engraving of his residence, Schuylerville, N.Y. It appears, without the full margins, facing p. 260 of Nathaniel B. Sylvester, History of Saratoga County, N.Y. (Philadelphia: Everts and Ensign, 1878).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cabinet photograph , inscribed by the sitter to "my dear neice Alice," 1901.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolution in memory of CMM, late president, Saratoga Victory Manufacturing Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence about the family plot in Prospect Hill Cemetery, Schuylerville, 1956-67, including a plot plan and inscriptions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous materials, including "Constitution and By-laws / 19567-1957," along with other materials accumulated by Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, including a newspaper photograph of her as a founding member on its 45th anniversary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters sent, 1963-67, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, except for one of 1967 sent to Mr. and Mrs. Alvin R. Carpenter; family news.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter sent to Ruth Everett, 1965.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An issue of The Genealogical Advertiser, vol. 4, no. 1 (March 1901), containing an article, "English Ancestry of Governor Thomas Mayhew."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cards received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Estate papers, 1966-67. Includes legal papers; an exchange of letters between Conrad and Lucy Meinecke, Springville, N.Y. with Alvin and Betty Carpenter, Binghamton, N,Y, 1967, in reference to Alan Wickham's financial irresponsibility; with a 1961 appraisal of the family residence at 129 Murray Street, including a Polaroid photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Hospital mail," 1966.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters received, 1914-41.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters received, 1963-65.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Medical documents, 1951-66.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings, 1946-64, relating to the Monday Afternoon Club, the Shakespeare Dramatic Club and other theatrical events, politics, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1882-89.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: School reports, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1882-89.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers prepared for the Saturday Study Club, Schuylerville, 1901-02

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph marked "Bob Wickham's baby sister."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Biographical Sketch of the National Progressive Party's nominee for Broome County Judge and Surrogate." Pamphlet, n.d. [?1912] 2 pp. Contains a biography of the subject along with useful biographical data on his father, Charles H. Wickham.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Financial papers, 1917-58; includes record of income, 1917-25; status, 1956; and utility recapitulation, 1957-58.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Law licenses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Medical papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pre-academic certificates, Saratoga Springs Union School, 1893-96.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodcraft materials, clipped from newspapers and magazines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ephemera, 1928-70. Includes church bulletins from Trinity Memorial Church (Episcopal), Binghamton; insurance policies; junk mail; and unclassifiable material. An important item is "My constant prayer," in the hand of RSW.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Original Sketches of Miss Anne E. Allaben for 'A Saratoga Boyhood' by Robert S. Wickham. (Final proofs of all plates made therefrom opposite.)" Scrapbook with pencil drawings and photo prints pasted in. A 2 pp. carbon copy of a review by Arthur C. Parker of Naples, N,Y. [a prominent Seneca and archaeologist retired from the Rochester Museum of Arts and Sciences] is laid in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Constitution, by-laws, rules, membership list, annual report, list of allotments with surveys and floor plans. No Carpenter or Mayhew is listed, nor any resident of Saratoga Springs; the club was three years old,

did.unittitle: An Act, to Organize the Militia of the State of New-York, Passed April 21, 1818. Albany: J.W. Clark
Amendments to the Militia Law, Passed March 13, 1821. Albany: Websters and Skinners, n,d,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marked in ink "Simon DeRidder," along with pp. 7-10 of another copy marked "Walter DeRidder."

did.unittitle: Annual Register of the United States Naval Academy at Annapolis, Md. 1875-76. Washington: Government Printing Office
Laws of New-York: viz. An Act, to amend an Act, entitled "an Act to organize the Militia of this State" . . . Albany: J. Buel, [1814]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete.

did.unittitle: Official Register of the Officers and Midshipmen of the United States Naval Academy. Newport, Rhode Island. Nov. 1st, 1864. Newport: Frederick A. Pratt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The list of midshipmen forming the fourth class of 1864 (p. 23) includes Alvin R. Carpenter from "Texas." He initialed inside front cover, and made notes about the fate of his classmates. A pamphlet, "Regulations Governing the Admission of Candidates into the Naval Academy," is inside the front cover.

did.unittitle: To the Republican Electors of the State of New-York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet, uncut

did.unittitle: The Book of Common Prayer. London: Eyre and Spottiswoode

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pocket format. Inscribed on flyleaf: "Robert S. Wickham / Binghamton / N.Y."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Saratoga Boyhood, wrapped and tied for mailing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class of 1928 75th Reunion paperweight for Most Cornell Descendants

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class of 1928 65th Reunion medal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blueprints, two sheets, 1915.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Certificates, 1928-42 and n.d. Includes a swimming certificate from Cornell, diplomas and certifications, and a tribute upon retirement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers. Included are two slave indentures, 1827; commencement program, Union College, 1818; two commissions, Walter DeRidder, 1818, and another, 1819; "An Oration on Noses," n,d,; several documents on the division of WDR's estate, 1832; letter, E.H. Dixon, House of Refuge, to Jacob Deridder, "Opposite Schuylervile, "1832, on the occasion of Walter's death; deed, Jesse Chatfield to Kilian DeRidder, 1776; officers' roll of Col. Walter DeRidder's Regiment, New York State Militia; and a small receipt for eggs and silk hose.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commission, Columbia County Light Infantry, 1809.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue of June 11, 1914 with front-page obituary of Alvin R, Carpenter; four copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diploma, Binghamton Junior High Schools, 1929.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diploma, Schuylerville Union School, 1889.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diploma, Saratoga Springs Union Free School, 1896.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Builder's Estimate and Time Book, 1912. Only one week was used, for 11 March 1912 at 1134 West 37 Drive, apparently in greater Los Angeles. Seven workers' hours are recorded including "CMC" and "Hirashima."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary of her European travels "with brother" from December 25, 1877 to July 16, 1878. Slipped in are clipped engravings and clippings, apparently of sites visited: Eaton Hall, Hotel and Pension de l'Ours at Grindelwald, Grand Hotel Pension Bellevue au Lac Zurich, Hotel du Nord, Coeln, and Hotel Monet, Vevey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Partial copy of the previous item.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commonplace book, 1835, with transaction entries of a farmer. Difficult to decipher; commodities include eggs, apples, pork, ham; there is a reference to "Troy and Champlain ofice" and another to "wish to draw wood Cambrge."

did.unittitle: Mayhew, Ida C., Victory Mills, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exercise book, Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass., 9 October 1862, later used as a clipping book, with newspaper items dated 1862 to 1873. Inside cover is a photograph inscribed "Maplewood Hotel / Pittsfield / Mass / Formerly Maplewood Institute."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account book, 1932-37, of household and personal expenditures at item level. Inside cover is a membership list of a professional women's organization, the "New York Epsilon Athenians – Binghamton, N.Y."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Physics notebook, n.d., probably used at Saratoga Springs High School, but possibly at Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book of reviews, letters, etc. of A Saratoga Boyhood (1948), including a short errata (p. 4) and a fine letter (p. 5) from Frances Steloff in which she comments that she was the "official flower girl" in Saratoga until she left for Boston in 1900. A press release and an order blank are slipped in.

did.unittitle: "Aunt Fanny." Night Cap Stories. Boston: Estes and Lauriat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed inside front cover: "To Alvin / From / Georgina / Merry Xmas / 1914."

did.unittitle: An Impartial Narrative of the Most Important Engagements Which Took Place Between His Majesty's Forces and the Rebels, During the Irish Rebellion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First American Edition. Cambridge, N,Y: Tennery and Stockwell, n.d. Inscribed "Simon DeRidder" on p. 3.

did.unittitle: Luce. S.B. Seamanship. New York: D. Van Nostrand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed in ink on flyleaf: "A.R. Carpenter / Midshipman / U.S.N. / 1864." Covers missing

did.unittitle: Redfield, Henry S. Civil Procedure. Selected Pleadings. No. I. Cornell University College of Law

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed on cover: "Robert Sloane Wickham."

did.unittitle: Class 1864-1868 / United States Naval Academy / The Fiftieth Anniversary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet, two copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives for albums in boxes 9-10. "Karl B. Stickley" [hereafter KBS], 59 Carroll Street Marked in pencil "1916" 1-5 prints 6-38 negatives

scopecontent.p: Marked in pencil "Best East Inlet hunting trip 1919 films – McComb, etc." 1-6 negatives

scopecontent.p: Rubber stamped "AUG 6 1920" 1-5 prints 6-11 negatives

scopecontent.p: 1-2 prints 3-13 negatives Notes: 5, curious wooden church; 8, automobile

scopecontent.p: Marked in pencil "Niagara et al 1922" 1-67 negatives Notes: 18, curious wooden church; 57-58, "Spirit of Life," Saratoga Springs

scopecontent.p: Marked in pencil "1923" 1-8 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marked In pencil "Limekiln Falls / 1926" 1-6 negatives

did.unittitle: Robert S. Wickham, U.S. District Court, Binghamton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1-29 negatives Notes: 29, dog

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1-27 negatives

did.unittitle: Robert S. Wickham, U.S. District Court, Binghamton

scopecontent.p: Marked in pencil "Otter Brook trip 1932" 1-3 prints 4-13 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marked in pencil "1938" "A. Wickham" and rubber stamped "AUG 3 193[ ]" 1-2 prints 3-24 negatives Notes: 1, marked "Beaver Lake" and stamped "SEP 1 1938"; 2, stamped same

did.unittitle: "Randall's Photo Finishing Service / Binghamton Savings Bank"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings 1-13 negatives Notes: 7-12 seem to be on a cruise; 10 bears signs "Ferry to Inverrurie – Belmont" and "Apothecaries Hall" [perhaps in Aberdeen, Scotland]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1-9 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marked on reverse "Ford / Knickerbocker / 19 St. Charles Place" 1-25 prints 26-28 negatives Notes: 13, 3 copies with marking from negative, Doc"; 14, marking from negative "Boom" (shows a cannon); 15-17, caricatures identified of immediate family; 20, "A Dead Dog"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stamped "SEP 15 1920" 1-10 prints 11 negative Notes: 1-2, curious church; 11, negative of same

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings 1-10 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marked on reverse "Moses Lake / Quaker Lake" and stamped JUL 20 1920" 1-6 prints 7-11 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings, but license plate on automobile reads 1920 1-28 prints 29-34 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings 1-11 prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings 1-5 prints 6-8 negatives Notes: 1, marked on reverse "Helen Colclazer" (appears to be a c.1890 print); 2, marked "Eloise Cameron Carpenter" with photographer imprint "George W. Conkey / Glens Falls" (baby photo); 36, Saratoga Monument

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marked in pencil "Discards" 1 print 2-61 negatives Notes: 1, man with backpack; 2, Ondawa Hotel [Schroon Lake]; 3, motor stage; 4, building with sign "Homemade Candy"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 print 2-30 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings, but license plate on automobile reads 1917 1-93 negatives Notes: 4, above mentioned auto; 46, interior of cabin; 50, hotel, perhaps AuSable Club; 64, cabin; 68, house; 81, interior; 86, five women on a porch; 88-89, dog portrait; 90,93, boy on city street; 91, girl on city street

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings, but license plate on automobile reads 1917 1-93 negatives Notes: 4, above mentioned auto; 46, interior of cabin; 50, hotel, perhaps AuSable Club; 64, cabin; 68, house; 81, interior; 86, five women on a porch; 88-89, dog portrait; 90,93, boy on city street; 91, girl on city street

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No markings 1-5 prints 6-46 negatives Notes: 12, auto; 13, David Henderson monument near Tahawus; 19-20, hiking with packs; 29, city street; 34, building with sign "Trading Post"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Marked in ink with RSW's print order 1-11 negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unmounted prints taken by a competent amateur, with a few mounted and/or professional photographs, as follows:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mary (Hendy), Alvin R. II, and Alice Carpenter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly American Gothic stone buildings.vvvvvvvvvvv

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some are family homes, some historic houses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes baseball and football games, Asian circus performers, vehicles, and several interiors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Views probably in Great Britain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly groups; unidentified.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably unidentifiable, many with water, possibly the Hudson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Congress Park, streetcar, overturned railroad car, village from above, and a substantial school building.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carte de Visite Album, probably of Alvin R. and Alida (Mayhew) Carpenter of Schuylerville, N.Y. Many photographs were identified on the reverse, and these markings have been copied in pencil to the pages themselves, where there were already some captions. See especially four-year-old girl with rifle (p. 13), man with flute (p. 15), boy in Western Union messenger uniform (p.18). Carpenter family members: Meliza (Rich) Carpenter, Alida (Mayhew) Carpenter, Eloise Carpenter, Alvin R. Carpenter, Frank W. Rich Mayhew family members: Emily W. of Detroit, Portia of Detroit DeRidder family members: J.H. DeRidder of Springfield Friends: "son of Enoch Mudge of Boston"; Walter Edward Houghton (b. 1871) of Brooklyn; Howard Russell (b, 1876) of New Bedford; Charles Abram Russell (b, 1877); Benjamin D. Benson of New York City Faculty of Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass.: Mr. and Mrs. Spear, L.M. Johnson, Miss Gibbs, Mr. Feeder, Mr. Davenport Students of Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass.: Cora Coulter, Kitty Van Valkenburg, Hannah Durye/Burge, Mary Pettee, Oillie Allston (Chillicothe, O.), Lillie Whitman, Libbie H. Tupper (Hardwick, Mass.), Maggie Cook (Middletown, Conn.), Bell Stowe (Brooklyn), Millie Whitcomb (North Bridgewater, Mass.), Emily Nash (Troy), Sarah Edwards, Bell Morrison, Sarah Edwards (New York City), Emma W. Hubbard (Northampton, Mass.), Fanne Mather, Minnie Dayton, Georgie Masters, Mary Davis, Dotti DeFreest Students at the Naval Academy: S.D. Ames, John P. Wallis, Pat McAllister, C.M. Harris, R. Mitchell, Lieut. Bishop (instructor), E.C. Bartlett, L.S. Shook, H.C. [?Stinson], Clark Smith, Babe Doty, W.C. Strang/Strong.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1 of 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 2 of 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers read before the Shakespeare Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regimental record book, 1812. Contains orders, muster rolls, and returns. The book had been "Sloop Pollys Account Book," 1792, but only the records inside the front cover remain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Retained by donor Wickham, Robert S. Family photograph album, c. 1860-1956. A fine collection of family photos, many of them identified. This album was assembled using rubber cement which has entirely dried up - please use CAUTION when turning pages.
